records.ety: [L. parodia, Gr. parw,di`a; para` beside + 'w,dh` a song: cf. F. parodie. See Para-, and Ode.]

defs: A writing in which the language or sentiment of an author is mimicked; especially, a kind of literary pleasantry, in which what is written on one subject is altered, and applied to another by way of burlesque; travesty.
quotes: The lively parody which he wrote . . . on Dryden's “Hind and Panther” was received with great applause.

defs: A popular maxim, adage, or proverb.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. parodied (?); p. pr. & vb. n. parodying.]

defs: To write a parody upon; to burlesque.
quotes: I have translated, or rather parodied, a poem of Horace.
